item.title: Carney and Poilievre find rare common ground on the global stage
item.description: They may be bitter rivals in the House of Commons, but outside the chamber, recent events may have led to Conservative Leader Pierre Poilievre and Prime Minister Mark Carney forging a bond.

item.title: Carney meets world: Is prime minister a more international job now?
item.description: In total, Mark Carney spent 84 days either travelling or visiting other countries in his first year as prime minister. According to records provided by Global Affairs Canada, Justin Trudeau's first year included 63 days of travel. For Stephen Harper, the total was 34 days.

item.title: NDP leadership hopefuls make final push ahead of convention vote
item.description: With one week until New Democrats choose a new federal leader, the front-runner in the race says he's preparing a transition team and hopes the party can use his campaign machinery in the next election.

item.title: Europe is eyeing France's nuclear shield. Should Canada follow?
item.description: As European allies quietly explore France's nuclear umbrella amid doubts about U.S. reliability, Canada faces an uncomfortable question: join or stay clear. While Ottawa rejects going nuclear, extending French deterrence to North America risks inflaming tensions with Washington — and raises stark questions about who would defend whom in a crisis.

item.title: Canadian military personnel leave Iraq with NATO allies amid Iran war
item.description: The alliance said Friday it has safely relocated to Europe the last of its personnel from Mission Iraq, a non-combat advisory operation.

item.title: Ont. ticket price cap hard to enforce, could send fans to informal markets: experts
Canada has deported only one Iran official — here’s what we know about those efforts
item.description: Diaspora groups have long called on Ottawa to deport people who have served as regime officials in Iran.

item.title: Service down on stretch of Eglinton Crosstown LRT, no estimated time of return
Toronto council asked to oppose Doug Ford’s push to bring jets to Billy Bishop airport: ‘It’s our waterfront’
Toronto Argonaut turned military outfitter wants Canadian Army to be ‘brilliant at the basics’
Sikh organization calls for hate crime probe in killing of former Brampton resident on Alberta highway
Canadian Armed Forces members among NATO troops pulled out of Iraq
item.description: Canadian military members and civilians are among the personnel NATO has pulled out of Iraq as the country faces retaliatory attacks from Iran along with other Gulf countries during the U.S.-Israeli war on Iran.

item.title: Three youths charged after GO train assault, two victims pushed off train
Olivia Chow wants to ban ICE agents during FIFA World Cup in Toronto
item.description: Canada is expected to draw one million visitors including athletes, coaches, officials, volunteers and fans.

item.title: Doug Ford wants to outlaw resale tickets that cost more than the original price
Ontario ‘actively exploring’ stricter e-bike, e-scooter rules, says transportation minister
item.description: At a press conference Friday, Prabmeet Sarkaria said the government has to ensure the rules keep up with quickly evolving technology.

item.title: The Globe and Mail leads in National Newspaper Awards nominations
item.description: Senior international correspondent Mark MacKinnon, Calgary reporter Carrie Tait and health reporter Alanna Smith are nominated in multiple categories

item.title: A ‘lone actor’ attack on Jewish Canadians is ‘realistic’ risk: report
item.description: Canada’s Integrated Threat Assessment Centre says the U.S.-Israeli war against Iran has heightened an already present threat against Jewish Canadians and public officials.

item.title: ‘Your days of ripping people off are done:’ Ontario to ban resale of tickets for more than face value
item.description: Ontario is moving to outlaw the resale of tickets for more than their original value ahead of FIFA World Cup games in Toronto that are expected to carry significant demand.

item.title: National Defence confirms Canadians on exchange with U.S. not deployed to Gulf
item.description: The Department of National Defence confirms nearly three dozen Canadian military members on exchange with a division of the U.S. Air Force have not deployed to the Persian Gulf region.

item.title: Randy Hillier's convoy case back on after stay of charges overturned
item.description: The criminal case against former Ontario politician Randy Hillier over his participation in 2022's convoy protest in Ottawa is back on after the province's top court overturned a decision to stay the charges.

item.title: Quebec premier dismisses Doug Ford’s concerns over province’s EV targets
Edmonton man wanted in two fatal GTA shootings arrested in Calgary
3 Quebec maritime trainees return home after being stranded aboard ship in the Persian Gulf
item.description: A Quebec-based shipping company says three Canadians have returned home after being stuck aboard their vessels in the Persian Gulf.

item.title: Pierre Poilievre and Joe Rogan talk a little bit about politics and so much about kettlebells
item.description: In between extended meditations on all things bro, we learned some interesting things about Mr. Poilievre

item.title: Elizabeth Renzetti: Doug Ford’s tried everything to keep his secrets. Changing the law is his last refuge
Support for Ford’s Tories has levelled off after months of decline, poll suggests
Queen’s Park statues getting wrapped to prevent vandalism at upcoming protest
Ontario to miss federal deadline for publicly funding nurse practitioners
item.description: Ontario will not have a policy in place to publicly fund all medically necessary services from nurse practitioners by April 1, as ordered by the federal government, leaving some patients paying out of pocket for primary care.

item.title: Ontario to miss deadline to fund nurse practitioners, leaving some patients paying out of pocket
item.description: Federal government has given the April 1 deadline, but provinces won’t start incurring penalties for non-compliance until April, 2027

item.title: From oil and tariffs to kettlebells: Five highlights from Pierre Poilievre on Joe Rogan
Attack against Canadian Jewish community a ‘realistic possibility,’ federal report warns
item.description: Integrated Threat Assessment Centre says violent extremism could happen in the next six months

item.title: Refugee family faces separation as father and son ordered deported from Canada
item.description: An Indian man whose wife is an accepted refugee in Canada is facing deportation with the couple's five-year-old son in what lawyers say is a troubling new practice of separating the families of people with protected status.

item.title: Customers complain about long wait times, multiple calls to resolve issues with Rogers, Telus and Bell
item.description: More than a dozen customers told Marketplace they are frustrated with poor customer service they received from Canada’s big three telecoms. Employees at Rogers and Telus said frontline customer service representatives have less incentive to help customers issue credits or lower bills, and said they’re measured on their abilities to increase customers’ bills.

item.title: Pierre Poilievre frames himself as prime-minister-in-waiting on whirlwind U.S. tour
The murky question and answers on whether India is still meddling in Canada continue
item.description: The RCMP Commissioner is not making matters much clearer

item.title: Online Streaming Act targeted by U.S. Republicans in move that could lead to new tariffs
item.description: Bill introduced in U.S. Congress would investigate whether Canada’s legislation discriminates against or unfairly burdens American companies

item.title: Politics Insider: Canada, European allies, Japan willing to use efforts to reopen Strait of Hormuz
item.description: And, Poilievre pushes back on Joe Rogan’s comments about oil sands during podcast appearance

item.title: Six takeaways from Pierre Poilievre’s appearance on Joe Rogan’s podcast
item.description: Conservative Leader discussed MAID, tariffs and Trump’s 51st state comments during the interview on Thursday

item.title: Republican bill takes aim at Online Streaming Act and threatens retaliation
item.description: The legislation from U.S. Rep. Lloyd Smucker would launch a U.S. Trade Representative investigation into whether the Canadian law unfairly burdens American commerce.

item.title: 7 key takeaways from Poilievre's Joe Rogan interview
item.description: Conservative Leader Pierre Poilievre sat down for a more than two-hour-long interview on The Joe Rogan Experience this week. Here are some key takeaways from the discussion.

item.title: U.S. congressman targets Canada's 'Netflix tax' with new bill
item.description: A new Republican-sponsored bill in the U.S. Congress takes aim at Canada's attempts to force foreign streaming companies such as Netflix and Disney+ to pay into Canadian funds for domestic content production.

item.title: Supreme Court of Canada reserves decision on landmark Facebook data-harvesting case
item.description: The Supreme Court of Canada is delaying its ruling on a landmark privacy case involving Facebook's role in the 2010s Cambridge Analytica scandal.

item.title: Shared kitchens, food hubs can reduce barriers for startups: Competition Bureau
Poilievre pitches Canadian kindness on ‘The Joe Rogan Experience’ podcast
item.description: Poilievre recorded the episode while in Austin, Texas recently, where he also met with Governor Greg Abbott.

item.title: Canada, allies ready to help reopen the Strait of Hormuz
item.description: Defence Minister says Canada is ‘considering’ aiding Iran’s neighbours if they seek assistance from NATO alliance

item.title: Defence Minister walks back comments on learning about Iranian strike from newspaper report
item.description: David McGuinty earlier said he learned about potential damage to Canadian assets in Kuwait from a La Presse report

item.title: Ontario to try again to put patient health records online
item.description: A previous Liberal government's attempt to create a system with its ill-fated eHealth agency cost $1 billion and offered minimal results.

item.title: To chop spending, Ottawa will cut science, tourism, foreign aid programs
item.description: While 40 departments will collectively spend $23 billion more next year, another 85 departments will spend $31 billion less in 2026-27.

item.title: Canadian population saw 2nd straight drop as 2025 ended: StatCan
item.description: Statistics Canada states that as of Jan. 1, 2026, Canada's population was 41,472,081, sitting 'well below the levels observed in the fourth quarters of 2023 and 2024.'

item.title: Poilievre pushes back on Joe Rogan’s comments about oil sands during podcast appearance
item.description: Conservative Leader also used interview with U.S. podcaster to make the case for Americans to drop tariffs on Canadian imports

item.title: Ontario plans to replace disjointed electronic medical records with its own system for family doctors
item.description: The province says plans are in the works to develop its own electronic medical records system for family doctors that will link the “disconnected” software that currently prevents physicians from sharing information with other health-care professionals.

item.title: U.S. hasn’t formally asked NATO to help keep Strait of Hormuz open, Anita Anand says
Poilievre, Rogan chat MMA, exercise and trade: ‘Knock the tariffs down’
item.description: The Conservative leader made an appearance on The Joe Rogan Experience podcast after turning down Rogan’s invite during the last election.

item.title: Canada is letting rural employers hire more temporary foreign workers. Economists say it’s a misstep
Supreme Court to hear challenge of federal firearms ban
item.description: Government banned the use and sale of more than 1,500 firearms models and variants in 2020, saying they were only fit for the battlefield

item.title: Canada fast-tracks army rifle upgrade with $307M initial order
item.description: Canada is fast-tracking the purchase of more than 65,000 new assault rifles, starting with a $307 million order for 30,000 weapons. Built by Colt Canada, the rifles will replace aging C7 and C8 models, marking a push to modernize the army and speed up procurement through a streamlined process.

item.title: Canada spending $307M to buy new army rifles from Colt
Doug Ford suggests judge should apologize to Toronto cops in Umar Zameer case — ‘That’s the first step’
Ottawa earmarks $1.4 billion for key munitions production in Quebec, Ontario
Canada Revenue Agency shuttering drop box filing system after tax season closes
Toronto's donor-funded consumption sites bracing for fallout of Ontario funding cuts
item.description: As the Ontario government cuts funding for seven supervised drug consumption sites in the province, workers at three remaining sites in Toronto that don’t rely on provincial funding say they’re worried the move will further strain their resources and lead to more overdoses and open drug use across the city.

item.title: Time to plan for the return of sane immigration
item.description: Recent past does not teach us that immigration is bad, but that wild swings in immigration policy are bad

item.title: Here are Canada's biggest points of leverage in tariff and trade talks with the U.S.
item.description: While Canada's economy is far more reliant on exports to the U.S. than vice versa, Canadian negotiators have crucial ammunition in their efforts to land a trade deal that reduces or eliminates tariffs imposed by U.S. President Donald Trump.

item.title: Departmental plans fuel concern over federal job, program cuts
item.description: Federal programs aimed at addressing climate change are facing significant cuts as the government shifts to artificial intelligence and slashes thousands of jobs, according to recently released departmental plans.

item.title: Poilievre says he used Joe Rogan podcast to argue against U.S. tariffs and 'fight for Canada'
item.description: Conservative Leader Pierre Poilievre is set to appear on The Joe Rogan Experience, one of the world's most popular podcasts.

item.title: Statues on grounds of Queen’s Park to be wrapped in the wake of being vandalized during protest
item.description: The statues on the ground of Queen’s Park will be covered in temporary wrapping after some of them were vandalized by protesters earlier this month.

item.title: Poilievre to appear on Joe Rogan’s podcast after turning it down during last election
item.description: Influential U.S. podcaster’s interview with the Conservative Leader is expected to air on Thursday afternoon

item.title: Politics Insider: Bank of Canada holds its benchmark interest rate steady
item.description: And, Canada’s population declined by more than 100,000 people in 2025, marking the first annual decline in records that date back to the 1940s

item.title: Alberta moves to drastically reduce access to medically assisted dying
item.description: The UCP government introduced a bill stipulating only those likely to die of natural causes within a year would be eligible for medical assistance in dying.

item.title: ‘I’m glad you shot the guy:’ Doug Ford praises Vaughan resident who opened fire on intruder
item.description: Premier Doug Ford is congratulating a Vaughan resident who shot an intruder, saying he should have fired more shots at him.

item.title: Quebec projects $8.6-billion deficit in election-year budget
item.description: Province’s budget focuses on core services like health and education with no flashy big-ticket spending

item.title: Global Affairs Canada eyes taking ‘more risks’ in uncertain world: document
item.description: The foreign affairs department is struggling under Prime Minister Mark Carney’s cuts, and now is 'challenging traditional assumptions' about its work.

item.title: Thousands attend pro-Palestinian rally in Toronto after judge denies Ford government’s injunction to stop it
Rideau Cottage 'inadequate' home for a PM as decision on 24 Sussex looms: internal memo
item.description: Rideau Cottage, the historic red-brick house where Canadian prime ministers have been living for more than a decade, is inadequate and comes with security risks, according to a government memorandum prepared last summer.

item.title: Mark McLane, cabinet minister in P.E.I., dies at 56
item.description: Premier Rob Lantz paid tribute to the late minister, saying McLane was ‘one of a kind’

item.title: Doug Ford praises homeowner who shot man during home invasion: ‘These guys, they need to be shot’
item.description: "Congratulations for shooting this guy — should have shot him a couple more times, as far as I'm concerned," the premier said Wednesday.

item.title: Alberta’s Smith defends taking private flight on behalf of Saudi government last fall
item.description: Premier and members of her staff travelled to discuss potential collaborations on energy development, AI and more

item.title: Making ammunition in Canada ‘essential’ for independence: defence minister
item.description: Defence Minister David McGuinty announced the launch of the Canadian Defence Industry Resilience Program Wednesday, with a focus on making ammunition in Canada.

item.title: Ontario premier says electric vehicle mandates putting jobs at risk, urges Quebec, B.C. to scrap policies
item.description: Premier Doug Ford is calling on the premiers of both Quebec and British Columbia to end all electric vehicle mandates, arguing that the policies are “threatening the livelihoods of tens of thousands of Canadian workers.”

item.title: Doug Ford defends FOI changes, warns of enemies seeking government secrets
Man arrested for identity theft no longer with CRA, federal agency says
Time for ‘serious’ talks on how AI uses news media information: minister
item.description: Culture Minister Marc Miller says the government must have a serious conversation about AI systems’ use of news to ensure companies are acting responsibly.

item.title: Meet the fixer Carney called to save Canada-U.S. trade talks
item.description: The post Meet the fixer Carney called to save Canada-U.S. trade talks appeared first on The Logic .
